fix: eliminate unreachable None branches in glob_match

Replace `if let Some(first) = parts.first()` and
`if let Some(last) = parts.last()` with
`.copied().unwrap_or_default()` in `glob_match`.

At those call sites `parts.len() >= 2` is already guaranteed by the
early-return for `parts.len() == 1`, so the `None` arm of each
`if let Some` was structurally unreachable.  LLVM branch coverage
tracked those impossible arms as missed branches, keeping
`security.rs` at 94.12% (2 missed / 34 branches).  Switching to
`unwrap_or_default()` removes the branch points entirely, bringing
the file to 100% branch coverage (0 missed / 30 branches) and the
workspace total from 92.61% → 92.69%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feature new drop system (#1254)

* Add NPC drop type and loading logic

Introduce t_NpcItemDrop and add QuantityOfDrops + drop arrays to NPC types. Update LoadNpcInfoIntoCache and InitializeNpcFromInfo to read per-drop fields (ItemIndex, DropChance, Low/HighQuantityBound) from NPC config, ReDim the drops array when present, and Erase it when none are defined. This enables NPCs to hold multiple item drop definitions loaded from the NPC data.

* Update Declares.bas

* Rename NPC drops array and add assert

Rename t_Npc field Drops() to Drop() and update usages in LoadNpcInfoIntoCache accordingly (ReDim .Drop, populate .Drop(i), Erase .Drop). Also add a Debug.Assert NpcNumber <> 1638 before reading drop info. These changes fix inconsistent naming and align the loader with the updated structure, preventing mismatches when resizing or erasing the drops array.

* Refactor NPC drop system; remove global table

Replace the global drop table with per-NPC drop data and update drop logic. Added QuantityOfDrops and Drop() to t_Npc (plus related fields), removed GlobalDropTable and LoadGlobalDropTable, and stopped loading the old table. Refactored NPC death/drop flow: NPC_TIRAR_ITEMS now skips inventory drops when NPC uses new drop entries, NpcDropeo was renamed/refactored to NpcDropObj to support both legacy Quiza lists and the new Drop array, and DropObjQuest was renamed to NpcDropQuestObj with clearer early exits. Updated NPC initialization (LoadNpcInfoIntoCache / InitializeNpcFromInfo) to populate QuantityOfDrops and Drop entries. Also small naming/capitalization and cleanup changes (e.g. spawn message name property). These changes migrate drop behavior to the new per-NPC system and remove the old global drop mechanism.

* Update Modulo_InventANDobj.bas

* Refactor NPC drop parsing; remove drop sound

Removed the t_GlobalDrop type and refactored NPC drop handling.

- Declares.bas: Deleted unused t_GlobalDrop type.
- MODULO_NPCs.bas: Read drop data from a single 'DropN' string and parse fields with ReadField('-', ...), and replaced element-wise copy with a direct .Drop = Info.Drop assignment to simplify initialization.
- Modulo_InventANDobj.bas: Removed SendData calls that played the drop sound when NPCs drop items.

These changes simplify drop data parsing, reduce duplicate assignments, and stop redundant sound sends on drops.

* Update Codigo/MODULO_NPCs.bas

Initial release: nested repository support preset for Spec Kit

Adds multi-module nested repository support for independent repos and git
submodules during the plan and tasks phases of spec-driven development.

Features:
- Auto-scan for independent .git directories with configurable depth
- Git submodule detection via .gitmodules parsing
- Auto mode that detects which type applies
- .gitignore-aware directory filtering during scan
- Type-aware branch creation tasks (git -C vs git submodule update)
- Configuration via .specify/init-options.json (optional)
- Full core plan/tasks workflow preserved with additive changes only

archive/tar, and html/template (GO-2026-4864 through GO-2026-4947),
all fixed in Go 1.26.2. govulncheck now passes cleanly.

commit.message:
fix: context window — max tokens 8192, history via system prompt, proactive reset at 75%

Three improvements:

1. DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS 2048 → 8192
   - Hardware profile already caps per tier: 8192 FLAGSHIP, 2048 MID,
     1024 LOW_POWER. The old default of 2048 was preventing flagship
     devices (S23 Ultra) from using their full context capacity.
     Root cause of model getting stuck repeating tokens.

2. History replay via system prompt (not user message prefix)
   - Extract buildSystemPrompt() helper that accepts optional history turns
   - On needsHistoryReplay or proactiveReset: call updateSystemPrompt()
     with history embedded as [Previous conversation context] block
   - Gemma treats system prompt as background context — far more reliable
     than prepending to the user message text

3. Proactive reset at 75% token budget
   - Track estimatedTokensUsed after each Complete generation event
   - At >75% of MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS, trigger reset + history replay
     before LiteRT can lock up and produce degenerate output
   - Counter reset on cancel, new conversation, and after replay

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove fastText support and enable numpy 2.x compatibility (#1315)

* Remove fastText support and enable numpy 2.x compatibility

FastText library has been archived since March 2024 and is no longer
maintained. This dependency was blocking numpy 2.x adoption.

Changes:
- Remove fasttext extra from setup.py dependencies
- Add deprecation warnings to fasttext.py module
- Update documentation to recommend langdetect as primary option
- Update CLI to deprecate --fasttext flag
- Add comprehensive test suite for langdetect integration
- Remove fasttext from tox.ini test configurations

Users should migrate to langdetect:
  pip install dateparser[langdetect]

Closes #1287
Closes #1251

* Update dateparser_cli/cli.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update dateparser/custom_language_detection/fasttext.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify fasttext deprecation to always error

The previous approach was confusing:
- If fasttext was installed, it showed a warning but still worked
- If fasttext wasn't installed, it raised an ImportError

This creates inconsistent deprecation - users don't know if the
feature is truly deprecated or not.

Now the module:
- Always shows deprecation warning on import
- Always raises ImportError when detect_languages() is called
- Provides clear migration path to langdetect
- No functional fasttext code remains

This makes the deprecation clear and consistent.

* Remove unused module-level mock in test_language_detect

The module-level 'detect_languages' mock was defined but never used.
The MockLangDetectTest class defines its own mock as a class attribute,
and other test classes use the actual lang_detect_detect_languages function.

This cleanup removes the unused mock to avoid confusion.
